description:
When both operands of an arithmetic op are literal-narrow (e.g. 1 + 1, or 2 * n where n is literal 3), the result Descr is widened to the base type (int, float) rather than the computed literal value.

Affected fixtures: hello, macro_inc, spec_ok, vr1_int_arith, vr3_4_typed_capture, vr4_2_native_call, vr5b_typed_print, add1

Example: add1(3) should produce a spec where the return is literally 4, but it shows int.

Fix: in ir_typer, when both operand Descrs are singleton literal sets of the same numeric type, evaluate the op at type-inference time and return the literal result Descr.

comments.text:
Investigation: naively folding in type_binop breaks recursive functions (fact tests fail). n-1 for n=int_lit(5) produces int_lit(4), creating a new narrow spec key [4] for fact. This cascades into [3],[2],[1],[0] before WIDEN_AT kicks in. These narrow specs get their comparisons folded too, and descrs_disjoint in codegen produces incorrect constant-folded branches.

Safe approach: only fold when both operands are IR-level constants (from Prim::Const), not from spec-key parameters. Requires threading const_vars: HashSet<Var> through type_prim. The const_vars set includes vars bound by Prim::Const(_) stmts plus any vars derived from const_vars via arithmetic (closure under const-derivation).

Comparison folding (compare_result) is already implemented and safe since boolean results don't create new numeric spec keys.

title: ir_typer: arithmetic on literal operands not constant-folded to literal result
